name: ADVENT TECHNOLOGIES HOLDINGS, INC.
business: Advent Technologies develops and manufactures High-Temperature Proton Exchange Membrane (HT-PEM) fuel cell technology, including Membrane Electrode Assemblies (MEAs) and fuel cell stacks. The company collaborates with global OEMs to produce complete fuel cell systems for stationary power (backup, off-grid, portable) and heavy-duty mobility (automotive, aviation, marine) applications. Revenue is derived from joint development agreements, technology licensing, and sales of MEAs and fuel cell systems.
sellsTo: stationary power markets (backup, off-grid, portable power)
namedSuppliers: BASF Environmental Catalyst and Metal Solutions

exposures.sentence: We are an advanced materials and technology development company operating in the fuel cell and hydrogen technology space, including liquid hydrogen carriers such as methanol.

Advent is working with global market-leading OEMs with the goal of bringing to the market complete fuel cell systems for a range of applications in the stationary power markets (backup, off-grid, and portable power) and the heavy-duty mobility markets (automotive, aviation, marine).
The recent JDA with Airbus showcases the potential of HT-PEM technology for the Aerospace sector.
The US Army development contracts for human portable power fuel cells also fall under this category.

Stationary Power for Data Centers, Microgrids, and Buildings: With a run-time of 200 hours per year (serving as backup) and typical power generator sizes ranging from 1 to 20MW, the total deployed capacity of power generators in data centers globally stands at 5.6GW.
Off-grid power solutions have the potential to run full-time, 365 days a year, 24 hours per day.

Our HT-PEM fuel cells and MEAs are well-suited to off-grid power, portable power applications, combined heat and power, and mobility (e.g., heavy-duty automotive, aviation, mining equipment, marine, and UAV).
The challenges associated with existing battery technology limit it from mass adoption across industries.
Marine applicationns are likely to develop initially in auxiliary power and smaller ships and eventually scale to the multi-MW range main propulsion market.
In December 2023, Advent demonstrated a portable clean power solution designed for various applications, including construction sites, remote EV charging stations, events, and concerts.

catalysts.event: Launch of RESCUE project for portable 50kW HT-PEM backup power generator
catalysts.sentence: The Company, through its wholly-owned subsidiary, Advent Technologies, SA, announced on February 11, 2025 the launch of the RESCUE project, a strategic initiative funded under the HORIZON-JTI-CLEANH2-2024-04-01 call for Portable Fuel Cells for Backup Power During Natural Disasters to Support Critical Infrastructures.

catalysts.event: RHyno Project awarded €34.5 million EU Innovation Fund grant
catalysts.sentence: On March 5, 2025, the European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency (CINEA) and Advent’s wholly owned Greek subsidiary, Advanced Energy Technologies S.A. each signed the grant agreement for the Company’s monumental RHyno Project.
On February 19, 2025, Advent and Airbus representatives met in Hamburg, Germany to kickoff Phase Two of the joint benchmarking project.
On March 23, 2023, Hyundai announced a successful technology assessment with Advent and following its success, Advent and Hyundai have entered into a Joint Development Agreement (JDA).

On May 9, 2023, Advent and BASF, a global leader in precious metals and catalysis, concluded on the terms of a new agreement to join efforts in building a closed loop component supply chain for fuel cells and enter discussions to extend the partnership into the field of water electrolysis.
On May 31, 2023, Advent signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Safran Power Units, a leader in auxiliary power systems and turbojet engines.

catalysts.event: Launch of Honey Badger 50 portable fuel cell system
catalysts.sentence: On August 4, 2022, we announced the launch of our HB50 power system, a compact portable fuel cell system and quiet power supply for use in off-grid field applications such as military and rescue operations.
In September 2023, Advent secured a new $2.2 million contract with the US DoD.
In December 2023, Advent secured a new $2.8 million contract with the US DoD.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable fuel cell market opportunity over $72 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: We believe the total addressable market opportunity could be over $72 billion by the year 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Average annual investment in hydrogen and fuel cell sector $38 billion between 2020 and 2040
tamClaims.sentence: Globally, an average of $38 billion per annum is expected to be invested in the hydrogen and fuel cell sector between 2020 and 2040 to increase production capacity while significantly lowering the cost of manufacturing.

tamClaims.claim: Global data center backup power generator capacity 5.6 GW
tamClaims.sentence: With a run-time of 200 hours per year (serving as backup) and typical power generator sizes ranging from 1 to 20MW, the total deployed capacity of power generators in data centers globally stands at 5.6GW.
